howItWorks.intro: This walking calorie calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Individual results vary based on metabolism, genetics, and activity level. Consult your doctor before starting any program.

faq.question: How many calories does walking burn?
faq.answer: A 170 lb person walking at 3.5 mph burns about 300 calories per hour. Faster pace and higher body weight increase the burn.

faq.question: How many steps in a mile?
faq.answer: Approximately 2,000 steps per mile at average stride length. Taller people take fewer steps, shorter people take more.

faq.question: Is walking good exercise?
faq.answer: Walking is excellent low-impact exercise. 30 minutes daily reduces heart disease risk by 19%, improves mood, and burns meaningful calories. 10,000 steps/day is a good target.

faq.question: Walking vs running for weight loss?
faq.answer: Running burns more calories per minute, but walking is sustainable for longer. Net calorie burn per mile is similar. Walking has lower injury risk.

faq.question: Is the Walking Calorie Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Walking Calorie Calculator?
sections.body: The Walking Calorie Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes and fitness enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the fitness and training space. By entering your weight, speed, duration, you get instant results including calories burned, distance, est. steps. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting calories burned right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In fitness and training, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes and fitness enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your training with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your weight and need to find the right calories burned. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your weight before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
